'''Nicholas Scruton''' (born 24 December 1984) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has previously played for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Hull F.C.]] and the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]]. Scruton has also previously represented [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Scruton was born in [[Morley, West Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==

===Hull F.C. (2004)===
In 2004 Scruton was loaned to [[Hull FC]]

He made 18 appearances and scored three tries in total.

===Leeds Rhinos (2002-08)===
Scruton was part of the [[Leeds Rhinos]]' [[2007 Super League Grand Final]] victory, over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].

Scruton was also part of the team that won the [[2008 World Club Challenge]], against the [[Melbourne Storm]] at [[Elland Road]]. He also played in the [[Super League XIII#Grand Final|2008 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].<ref name="2008 Grand Final">{{cite news|url= https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|title=2008 Grand Final|publisher=[[BBC News]]|date=4 October 2008|access-date=5 October 2008|url-status= live|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20081005210219/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|archive-date=5 October 2008 }}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (2009-14)===
Scruton signed a three-year deal to play for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] commencing in 2009.

In 2011, Scruton signed another three-year deal to extend his stay at the Bradford club.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.therfl.co.uk/news/article/scruton-signs-on-with-bulls|title=Scruton signs on with Bulls|publisher=[[Rugby Football League]]|date=28 March 2011|accessdate=10 February 2014|archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20110720050935/http://www.therfl.co.uk/news/article/scruton-signs-on-with-bulls|archivedate=20 July 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url= https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/12878776|title=Nick Scruton signs new Bradford Bulls deal|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|date=28 March 2011|accessdate=10 February 2014|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110720050935/http://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/12878776|archivedate=20 July 2011}}</ref>

In 2013, he collected many awards after the season with the Bradford side came to an end, where he won the 'Prized Bull Award,' from Head Coach, [[Francis Cummins]].<ref>{{cite news|url= http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/10653446.Some_fans_said_I_was_finished_but_look_at_me_now_____Scruton_is_Bradford_Bulls__comeback_king|title=Some fans said I was finished but look at me now – Nick Scruton is Bradford Bulls' comeback king|publisher=[[Telegraph & Argus]]|accessdate=29 October 2013|archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20110720050935/http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/10653446.Some_fans_said_I_was_finished_but_look_at_me_now_____Scruton_is_Bradford_Bulls__comeback_king|archivedate=20 July 2011}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats (2014-16)===
After Bradford went into [[liquidation]] for the second time in two-years in 2014, Scruton was then sold to [[Wakefield Trinity]] on a one-year deal.

===Hull Kingston Rovers (2017-19)===
Scruton joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] ahead of the 2017 [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] season on a two-year contract. Scruton was one of Hull Kingston Rovers standout players during the 2017 [[rugby league]] season, becoming a real 'fans' favourite' in his début year for Hull Kingston Rovers. Scruton was part of the Hull Kingston Rovers side that won promotion back to the [[Super League]], at the first time of asking following relegation the season prior.

It was revealed on 10 October 2018, that Scruton would be staying at Hull Kingston Rovers after signing a new one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|date=10 October 2018|title=Scruton staying on at Hull Kingston Rovers|url=http://mobile.twitter.com/hullkrofficial/status/1050083609120567298}}</ref>

After injuring himself in pre-season ahead of the start of the 2019 campaign, and not going onto make a single appearance in that season, it was revealed on 20 May 2019, that Scruton had been subsequently released from his contract at Hull Kingston Rovers.{{citation needed|date=January 2020}}

===Representative career===
===England (2004-06)===
Scruton has previously played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] on several occasions, he also recorded only a single try.

==Honours==
===Club===
'''Leeds Rhinos'''
*[[Super League]] (2): [[Super League XII|2007]], [[Super League XIII|2008]]
*[[World Club Challenge]] (1): [[2008 World Club Challenge|2008]]

'''Bradford Bulls'''
*[[2013 Bradford Bulls season|2013: Bradford Bulls]]' 'Prized Bull Award'
